description:
You are acting as a DevOps engineer who has to investigate and back-up application logs that are allegedly “on a remote host”.  
For the purpose of this exercise the “remote host” is emulated by the local directory

    /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/

while the local back-up location is

    /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/

Both paths already exist when you start.

1. Synchronise the two directories with rsync (or an equivalent tool) so that every “*.log” file located in  
   /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/  
   is copied to  
   /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/  
   while every file that matches the pattern “*.tmp” is explicitly excluded from the transfer.

2. Create the directory /home/user/sync_reports/ if it does not exist.

3. Immediately after the synchronisation finishes, generate a plain-text report file called  

       /home/user/sync_reports/2023-10-05_rsync_report.txt

   whose contents must follow *exactly* the template below (including blank lines, word casing, colons, dashes and ordering), substituting nothing—every line has to be identical to what is shown here:

       SYNC REPORT
       Date: 2023-10-05
       Source: /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/
       Destination: /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/
       Files considered: 4
       Files transferred: 3
       Excluded patterns: *.tmp
       Transfer status: SUCCESS

       List of transferred files:
       - app-2023-10-01.log
       - app-2023-10-02.log
       - debug-2023-10-01.log

       End of report

4. Verification requirements that the automated test suite will perform:
   • The directory /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/ must end up containing *only* the three “*.log” files listed above and **must not** contain session.tmp.  
   • Each copied file’s byte content must be identical to its counterpart in /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/.  
   • The report file /home/user/sync_reports/2023-10-05_rsync_report.txt must exist and match the template above byte-for-byte (no extra spaces or lines).  

No root privileges are available; accomplish everything as the current user. Display any console output you find helpful, but remember the automated tests check only the filesystem changes and the exact report file contents.
truth:
Initial filesystem state (before the agent starts):

1. Directories (with default 755 permissions):
   /home/user/remote_host_sim/
   /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/
   /home/user/local_backup/
   /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/

2. Files in /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/:
   a) /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/app-2023-10-01.log
      Content (including trailing newline): "LOG-A\n"
   b) /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/app-2023-10-02.log
      Content: "LOG-B\n"
   c) /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/debug-2023-10-01.log
      Content: "DEBUG-A\n"
   d) /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/session.tmp
      Content: "TEMP\n"

3. The directory /home/user/sync_reports/ does NOT exist initially.

Expected final state after correct task execution:

1. Directory /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/ contains exactly:
   a) app-2023-10-01.log with content "LOG-A\n"
   b) app-2023-10-02.log with content "LOG-B\n"
   c) debug-2023-10-01.log with content "DEBUG-A\n"

2. session.tmp is absent from /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/.

3. File /home/user/sync_reports/2023-10-05_rsync_report.txt exists with the following exact byte content:

SYNC REPORT
Date: 2023-10-05
Source: /home/user/remote_host_sim/app_logs/
Destination: /home/user/local_backup/app_logs/
Files considered: 4
Files transferred: 3
Excluded patterns: *.tmp
Transfer status: SUCCESS

List of transferred files:
- app-2023-10-01.log
- app-2023-10-02.log
- debug-2023-10-01.log

End of report

4. Permissions:
   • All newly created directories default to 755.
   • All newly created files default to 644.
